$comment_TEMPLATE: HARDEN STARTING ARTIFACT — the per-archetype, SCHEMA-SHAPED blueprint skeleton for a WIN-LOSE platformer (meta.goalModel == 'win-lose', the default branch). COPY this to spec/blueprint.json, then FILL every <FILL: ...> sentinel with your hardened values (SKILL.md §0.5). Every value below is a SENTINEL that FAILS blueprint.schema.json on purpose (a string where a number/enum/min-length-1 field is required, or an empty array where minItems applies), so an unfilled copy cannot validate. Each array shows ONE placeholder element exhibiting ALL its fields — REPLACE it with the real set (multiple entities/rewards/threats/platforms/milestones/ACs). This skeleton is a STARTING POINT, not a straitjacket: if classification's goalModel is 'open-ended' use templates/modules/voxel_sandbox/blueprint.template.json (or the open-ended branch per §3b/§4.4) instead. The OPTIONAL binding buckets (genre/controlScheme/effects/systems/custom/hud/subMode/anim) are present as scaffolds — keep what your design uses, delete the rest. Delete this $comment_TEMPLATE and every other $comment_* key before returning.

meta.title: <FILL: short game title, <=6 words>
meta.coreLoop: <FILL: one-line core loop carried from classification.json>
meta.coreVerb: <FILL: the core verb carried from classification.json>
meta.coreFantasy: <FILL: the player fantasy this game delivers (optional but recommended)>
meta.failModel: <FILL: enum one of health|lives|respawn|time|none — the resource the HUD surfaces>
meta.maxScore: <FILL: number — REQUIRED when scoringModel != none; == sum of layout.rewards values; OMIT this key entirely when scoringModel == none>
meta.artStyle: <FILL: global medium + palette + mood ONLY; name NO single subject/entity and NO per-entity color>
meta.assetBudget: <FILL: integer >= 1 — max real generated asset KINDS (default 12 for platformer); every primary visible KIND gets a real slot or you CUT SCOPE>

physicsProfile.perspective: <FILL: enum one of side|top_down|none|first_person — 'side' for a platformer>
physicsProfile.movementType: <FILL: enum one of continuous|grid|path|ui_only — 'continuous' for a platformer>

subMode: <FILL: OPTIONAL within-archetype variant hint for W4, or DELETE this key>

entities.id: <FILL: entity id, non-empty; the PLAYER must be entities[0] with role 'player'>
behaviors: <FILL: a behavior id resolving in this module's capabilities.json (kind=behavior), e.g. a movement behavior; OR replace this element with a {ref,params} binding object, OR a '$custom:<id>' string with a matching custom[] entry>

anim.<FILL: entity state key, e.g. idle/run>: <FILL: an assetList slot id for that state, or DELETE the anim object if unused>

entities.assetSlot: <FILL: this entity's assetList slot id, or DELETE if it has none>

mechanics.name: <FILL: mechanic name, non-empty>
mechanics.capability: <FILL: OPTIONAL capability id this mechanic maps to, or DELETE this key>

controls.input: <FILL: the physical input, e.g. a key or button, non-empty>
controls.action: <FILL: the game action it triggers, non-empty>

genre.ref: <FILL: a genre id from templates/genres.json whose archetype == 'platformer' (e.g. the base platformer genre or a subgenre); must resolve>
genre.subgenre: <FILL: OPTIONAL subgenre label from the genre record, or DELETE this key>

controlScheme.scheme: <FILL: a control-scheme id resolving in capabilities.json (kind=control-scheme)>

<FILL: an action name, e.g. jump>: <FILL: a key for that action, e.g. Space>

effects.on: <FILL: a real BUS event that triggers the effect — it MUST RESOLVE in platformer/events.catalog.json (∪ a custom[].emits[].name). Use the catalog's domain.pastTenseVerb names: enemy.died | enemy.damaged | reward.collected | player.damaged | player.died | player.shot | player.jumped | player.landed | player.respawned | hazard.activated | score.changed | level.statusChanged. NEVER an invented name (a bus the runtime never carries is a dead effect). A genuinely-new moment → declare it in a custom[].emits[] entry and key off that. non-empty>
effects.play: <FILL: an effect id resolving in capabilities.json (kind=effect), non-empty>

systems.ref: <FILL: a system id resolving in capabilities.json (kind=system), e.g. the collect->score logic or the score-gated-goal logic or the pure-completion goal logic; PREFER a systems[] binding over a custom[] system>

custom.id: <FILL: custom capability id, referenced as '$custom:<id>' from behaviors[]; ONLY for the genuinely-novel delta no resolving id covers>
custom.implements: <FILL: OPTIONAL the SDK interface the code targets, e.g. IBehavior, or DELETE>
custom.attachedTo: <FILL: OPTIONAL the entity id it attaches to, or 'scene' for a system, or DELETE>
custom.contract: <FILL: the OBSERVABLE behavior contract W4 implements — what it does to __GAME__ state / the world, non-empty>

emits.name: <FILL: a genuinely-new BUS event this custom component publishes (domain.pastTenseVerb) — W4's surface() declares + emits it; this is what lets an effects[].on / sfx[].on / guidance on-event keyed off it RESOLVE. DELETE the whole emits[] array if this component publishes no event a chrome producer reacts to>
emits.drivenBy: <FILL: the verb/moment that fires it (required when emits[] is present)>
emits.expect: <FILL: the observable transition it must cause (required when emits[] is present)>

custom.file: <FILL: OPTIONAL the single file W4 writes for this entry, or DELETE>

winCondition.description: <FILL: how the player wins, in plain terms, non-empty>
winCondition.observable: <FILL: the __GAME__ expression that is true on win, e.g. status === 'won', non-empty>

loseCondition.description: <FILL: how the player loses, in plain terms, non-empty>
loseCondition.observable: <FILL: the __GAME__ expression that is true on loss, e.g. status === 'lost', non-empty>

config.<FILL: a config-key id from this module': <FILL: number — the hardened value; config must be COMPLETE (every tunable the design needs) and every value a number>

assetList.slot: <FILL: the Phaser texture KEY for this asset, non-empty; one slot per primary visible KIND>
assetList.description: <FILL: a SUBJECT line (what it depicts + view direction) — NO style/palette/negatives; the W3a Art Director writes the full prompt>
frames: <FILL: for type 'animation' ONLY, an ordered frame id; else DELETE the frames key>
assetList.width: <FILL: integer >= 1 — logical display width px (per-frame for animations); the same box the §3 feasibility math uses>
assetList.height: <FILL: integer >= 1 — logical display height px (per-frame for animations)>

milestones.id: <FILL: milestone id matching ^M[1-9][0-9]*$, e.g. M1; supply 3-5 milestones total (min 3)>
milestones.name: <FILL: milestone name, non-empty>
milestones.goal: <FILL: the playable goal of this vertical slice, non-empty>
acceptanceCriteria: <FILL: a plain-language acceptance line for this milestone, non-empty; >=1 per milestone>

assertions.describe: <FILL: what this runtime check verifies, non-empty>

setup.scene: <FILL: OPTIONAL scene key to load before the check, or DELETE setup>

input.key: <FILL: OPTIONAL the key for keyPress/keyHold, or DELETE>
input.durationMs: <FILL: OPTIONAL integer >= 0 for keyHold, or DELETE>
input.target: <FILL: OPTIONAL the click/event target, or DELETE>

assertions.observe: <FILL: the __GAME__ field/path this assertion observes, e.g. status or score, non-empty>

expect.<FILL: EXACTLY ONE comparator key from d: <FILL: its value (boolean for the boolean comparators; number for atLeast/atMost; number|string|boolean for equals)>

playerSpawn.x: <FILL: number — player spawn x in world px; must sit INSIDE the start platform span and rest on its TOP (§6 self-critique)>
playerSpawn.y: <FILL: number — player spawn y in world px (rests on the start platform top)>

layout.groundSlot: <FILL: OPTIONAL the level-default ground/platform tile texture KEY (an assetList type 'tileset' slot); platforms without their own assetSlot tile this; or DELETE>

platforms.x: <FILL: number — platform CENTER x in world px (matches SDK createPlatform)>
platforms.y: <FILL: number — platform CENTER y in world px>
platforms.assetSlot: <FILL: OPTIONAL per-platform ground tile slot overriding groundSlot, or DELETE>

goal.x: <FILL: number — goal x in world px>
goal.y: <FILL: number — goal y in world px>

rewards.x: <FILL: number — reward x in world px>
rewards.y: <FILL: number — reward y in world px>

threats.x: <FILL: number — threat x in world px (for static_hazard/chaser; OMIT for a pure patrol that uses route)>
threats.y: <FILL: number — threat y in world px (as above)>

route.x: <FILL: number — waypoint x (patrol only; >=2 waypoints required; else DELETE route)>

threats.speed: <FILL: number > 0 — px/s (for patrol/chaser; keep the §3 passable window > 0)>
threats.periodMs: <FILL: number > 0 — full patrol cycle ms (patrol only; keep the §3 passable window > 0), or DELETE for a non-patrol kind>
threats.assetSlot: <FILL: OPTIONAL the hazard texture KEY (an assetList slot), or DELETE>

bounds.width: <FILL: number > 0 — world width px (the viewport width for one screen, or N x the viewport for a scrolling level; §2.1)>
bounds.height: <FILL: number > 0 — world height px (matches the viewport height)>

coupling.for: <FILL: the reward or goal id this entry contests (e.g. a reward id or the goal id), non-empty>
coupling.threat: <FILL: the threat entity id that contests it, non-empty>

meetsAt.x: <FILL: number — x where the player path intersects the threat region>

coupling.passableWindowMs: <FILL: number > 0 — the safe-reach timing window in ms (from §3 math); MUST be > 0 for a winnable+fair design>
coupling.note: <FILL: OPTIONAL human rationale — why the threat is on this path, or DELETE>

checks.what: <FILL: what was checked, e.g. a gap clearance or a reach time, non-empty; >=1 check required>

numbersUsed.<FILL: an input number key used in the c: <FILL: its number — record EVERY input so a human can re-run the arithmetic>

referenceSolution.winsVia: <FILL: how the win is achieved (the replay strategy for VERIFY-2), e.g. controls, non-empty>

steps.input: <FILL: the player action, e.g. a documented control hold/press with a duration, non-empty; >=1 step required>
steps.reaches: <FILL: OPTIONAL a landmark/coordinate reached after this step, or DELETE>
steps.clears: <FILL: OPTIONAL an obstacle/gap cleared, or DELETE>
steps.engagesThreat: <FILL: OPTIONAL the threat id engaged at this step (prove no threat is trivially avoided), or DELETE>
steps.observe: <FILL: OPTIONAL a __GAME__ observable to check after this step, e.g. status, or DELETE>
steps.expect: <FILL: OPTIONAL the expected value of that observable (string|number|boolean), used when observe is present, or DELETE>

referenceSolution.engagesEveryThreat: <FILL: boolean — true iff the reference solution engages every declared threat at least once>

acceptanceCriteria.milestone: <FILL: the milestone id this AC belongs to, matching ^M[1-9][0-9]*$, e.g. M1>
acceptanceCriteria.assertionId: <FILL: the gdd assertion id this AC upgrades, matching ^M[1-9][0-9]*-A[1-9][0-9]*$, in the SAME milestone, e.g. M1-A1>
acceptanceCriteria.given: <FILL: the precondition in concrete hardened coordinates so VERIFY-2 can reproduce the state, non-empty>
acceptanceCriteria.when: <FILL: the trigger/action, e.g. a documented control press, non-empty>
acceptanceCriteria.then: <FILL: the expected observable outcome as a __GAME__ expression, e.g. status === 'won', non-empty>
acceptanceCriteria.observable: <FILL: the __GAME__ field/path observed, e.g. status or score, non-empty>

expect.<FILL: EXACTLY ONE comparator key from d: <FILL: its value (boolean | number | number|string|boolean per the comparator)>

<FILL: a dotted parameterPath, e.g. conf: <FILL: number — band min; both endpoints MUST keep §3 feasibility + §4.1 threat-on-path true>
